IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: TO BE AN ADVOCATE FOR CHILDREN'S HEALTHCARE.
IRS990.MissionDesc: OUR CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL FOUNDATION IS DEDICATED TO SUPPORTING LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL'S MISSION TO BRING WHOLE-CHILD HEALTHCARE TO THE REGION AND BEYOND.
IRS990.Desc: THE MISSION OF LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL FOUNDATION IS TO ADVOCATE EXCELLENCE IN CHILDREN'S HEALTHCARE IN CALIFORNIA, THE UNITED STATES AND THE WORLD THROUGH THE SUPPORT OF THE LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL. TO ACCOMPLISH THIS THE FOUNDATION CONDUCTS VARIOUS FUNDRAISING ACTIVITIES AND COMMUNITY AWARENESS PROGRAMS.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, SCHEDULE A, SECTION D, LINE 3
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: INVESTMENT POLICIES AND USE OF ORGANIZATION'S INCOME OR ASSETS IS THE SAME POLICY AS THE SUPPORTING ORGANIZATION

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, SCHEDULE A, SECTION E, LINE 2A
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: THE MISSION OF LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL FOUNDATION IS TO ADVOCATE EXCELLENCE IN CHILDREN'S HEALTHCARE IN CALIFORNIA, THE UNITED STATES AND THE WORLD THROUGH THE SUPPORT OF THE LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL. TO ACCOMPLISH THIS THE FOUNDATION CONDUCTS VARIOUS FUNDRAISING ACTIVITIES AND COMMUNITY AWARENESS PROGRAMS

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, SCHEDULE A, SECTION E, LINE 2B
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL, HAD IT NOT BEEN FOR THE LOMA LINDA CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL FOUNDATION, WOULD HAVE ENGAGE IN SIMILAR FUNDRAISING AND AWARENESS PROGRAMS FOR THE ADVOCACY OF CHILDREN'S HEALTH.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE EXPLANATION BELOW IS TAKEN FROM THE "COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND SUPPLEMENTARY INFORMATION" FOR LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY MEDICAL CENTER AND AFFILIATES YEARS ENDED JUNE 30, 2024 AND JUNE 30, 2023. THE PRINCIPAL OPERATIONS OF LLUMC ARE EXEMPT FROM TAXATION PURSUANT TO INTERNAL REVENUE CODE SECTION 501(C)(3) AND THE RELATED STATE PROVISIONS. ACCOUNTING STANDARDS CODIFICATION (ASC) TOPIC 740, INCOME TAXES, CLARIFIES THE ACCOUNTING FOR INCOME TAXES BY PRESCRIBING A MINIMUM RECOGNITION THRESHOLD THAT A TAX POSITION IS REQUIRED TO MEET BEFORE BEING RECOGNIZED IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. ASC 740 ALSO PROVIDES GUIDANCE ON DERECOGNITION, MEASUREMENT, CLASSIFICATION, INTEREST AND PENALTIES, DISCLOSURE, AND TRANSITION. THE GUIDANCE IS APPLICABLE TO PASS-THROUGH ENTITIES AND TAX-EXEMPT ORGANIZATIONS. NO SIGNIFICANT TAX LIABILITY FOR TAX BENEFITS, INTEREST, OR PENALTIES WAS ACCRUED OR NEEDED TO BE ACCRUED AT JUNE 30, 2024 OR 2023. LLUMC CURRENTLY FILES FORM 990 (INFORMATIONAL RETURN OF ORGANIZATIONS EXEMPT FROM INCOME TAXES) AND FORM 990-T (BUSINESS INCOME TAX RETURN FOR AN EXEMPT ORGANIZATION) IN THE U.S. FEDERAL JURISDICTION AND THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA FOR EACH TAX-EXEMPT ORGANIZATION AS APPROPRIATE. LLUMC IS NOT SUBJECT TO INCOME TAX EXAMINATION PRIOR TO 2019 IN MAJOR JURISDICTIONS.
THE SMIDERLE, ANDERSON & WOOLLEY-PETTIS ENDOWMENTS ARE HELD IN PERPETUITY WITH THE INTEREST TO BE USED TO SUPPORT CHILDREN'S SERVICES AT LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL.

LLUCHF PROVIDES SPONSORSHIPS, DONATIONS, AND OTHER ASSISTANCE TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS AS PART OF ITS COMMITMENT TO COMMUNITY OUTREACH. DISBURSEMENTS FOR GRANTS AND OTHER ASSISTANCE ARE REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY MANAGEMENT PRIOR TO DISBURSEMENT. MANAGEMENT RECEIVES PERIODIC REPORTS REGARDING THESE ACTIVITIES AS PART OF THE COMMUNITY BENEFITS REPORT.
PLEASE SEE EXPLANATION FOR PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15 THAT IS LISTED UNDER SCHEDULE O.

THE FOLLOWING LLUH EMPLOYEES PARTICIPATED IN, AND RECEIVED PAYMENT FROM, A SUPPLEMENTAL NONQUALIFIED RETIREMENT PLAN: SERP FUNDING PAID TO EMPLOYEE IN CASH: - HUBBARD, MARK LEE: 251,779 NET FLEX PLAN CREDITS PAID TO EMPLOYEE IN CASH: - BAKER, PETER: 81,596 - BUSSELL, RACHELLE: 54,928 - EDWARDS, LYNDON C: 97,593 - THOMAS, TAMARA: 93,289 - HADLEY, HENRY ROGER: 23,019 - HANSEN, KENT: 97,711 - HART, RICHARD HENRY: 89,474 - MATUS, SARAH: 8,480 - HUBBARD, MARK L: 72,413 - JEAN-MARIE, JOHNATHAN: 45,994 - LALAS, ANGELA M.: 137,422 - LENO, SONDRA: 39,402 - NOLFE, SHERRY L.: 23,039 - OFT, DARYL: 40,705 - SODERBLOM, ALAN B.: 51,463 - VANDERBOSCH, DARRYL W.: 30,682 - WRIGHT, TREVOR: 154,926 - STAPLE-EVANS, HELEN: 81,345 TAX INDEMNIFICATION AND GROSS-UP PAYMENTS: - BAKER, PETER: 1,608 - BUSSELL, RACHELLE: 5,170 - EDWARDS, LYNDON C: 7,280 - HANSEN, KENT: 3,889 - HADLEY, HENRY ROGER: 11,008 - HART, RICHARD HENRY: 4,678 - SOUBIROUS, LINDA: 2,894 - HUBBARD, MARK LEE: 17,090 - JEAN-MARIE, JOHNATHAN: 1,034 - LALAS, ANGELA M.: 1,939 - THOMAS, TAMARA: 10,475 - LENO, SONDRA: 3,013 - NORTON, LIZETTE: 4,917 - SODERBLOM, ALAN B.: 2,141 - VANDERBOSCH, DARRYL W.: 3,897 - WRIGHT, TREVOR: 4,167 - OFT, DARYL: 448 - STAPLE-EVANS, HELEN: 7,262 EMPLOYER CONTRIBUTIONS/PAYMENTS FOR ELECTIVE BENEFITS (INCLUDES VESTED AND FORFEITED BENEFITS FUNDED IN PRIOR YEARS): - NONE

LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY MEDICAL CENTER A RELATED ORGANIZATION TO LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL FOUNDATION HAS AN INCENTIVE PLAN. PAYMENTS FOR INCENTIVE AWARDS ARE TRIGGERED ONLY IF, AMONG OTHER ESTABLISHED CRITERIA, NET INCOME EXCEEDS BUDGET BY AN ESTABLISHED PERCENTAGE.
ALL ITEMS DONATED AS PART OF OUR ANNUAL GALA FUNDRAISER FOR PROCEEDS BENEFITING LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL.

THE OFFICES OF PHILANTHROPY FOR LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY, LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY SHARED SERVICES AND LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL FOUNDATION ACCEPT MOST OF THE GIFTS FOR LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL FOUNDATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 2
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FAMILY RELATIONSHIPS MAY EXIST AMONG OTHER OFFICER'S, DIRECTOR'S, OR KEY EMPLOYEES OF OTHER RELATED ORGANZATIONS. THE NATURE OF THE BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP IS EMPLOYMENT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THIS CORPORATION SHALL HAVE ONLY ONE MEMBER AS THE "MEMBER" IS DEFINED IN SECTION 5056 OF THE CALIFORNIA CORPORATIONS CODE. THE SOLE MEMBER OF THIS CORPORATION SHALL BE LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY MEDICAL CENTER, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT RELIGIOUS CORPORATION (THE "CORPORATE MEMBER"). IN ACCORDANCE WITH SECTION 5056(C) OF THE CALIFORNIA CORPORATIONS CODE, THE CORPORATE MEMBER SHALL AUTHORIZE IN WRITING THREE (3) NATURAL PERSONS ("NATURAL PERSONS"), WHO SHALL ALSO BE TRUSTEES OF THE CORPORATE MEMBER, TO VOTE ON ITS BEHALF ON ALL MATTERS THAT MAY REQUIRE A VOTE OF THE CORPORATE MEMBER. SO LONG AS SUCH AUTHORIZATION REMAINS IN EFFECT, ALL ACTIONS TAKEN BY THE NATURAL PERSONS SHALL BE BINDING ON THE CORPORATE MEMBER. THE CORPORATE MEMBER SHALL NOTIFY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS IN WRITING OF ANY CHANGE IN THE NATURAL PERSONS AUTHORIZED TO ACT ON ITS BEHALF.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: A DIRECTOR MAY BE REMOVED FROM OFFICE WITH OR WITHOUT CAUSE BY A MAJORITY VOTE OF THE CORPORATE MEMBER. IN THE EVENT ONE OR MORE OF THE DIRECTORS IS SO REMOVED OR HIS OR HER OFFICE IS DECLARED VACANT, NEW DIRECTORS MAY BE ELECTED BY THE MAJORITY VOTE OF THE CORPORATE MEMBER TO FILL THE UNEXPIRED TERMS OF THE DIRECTORS WHO WERE REMOVED OR WHOSE OFFICES WERE DECLARED VACANT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE POWERS RESERVED TO THE CORPORATE MEMBER, WHICH MAY ONLY BE EXERCISED BY THE CORPORATE MEMBER UPON THE AFFIRMATIVE VOTE OF TWO OF THE THREE NATURAL PERSONS DESIGNATED TO ACT FOR THE CORPORATE MEMBER AS SET FORTH IN SECTION 4.1 ARE TO: 1) APPROVE OR RATIFY ALL CHANGES TO THE ARTICLES OF INCORPORATION OR BYLAWS OF THIS CORPORATION. 2) ELECT THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE CORPORATION. 3) REMOVE FROM OFFICE ANY DIRECTOR OF THE CORPORATION WITH OR WITHOUT CAUSE. 4) APPROVE THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR OF THE CORPORATION. 5) APPROVE THE ANNUAL BUDGET OF THE CORPORATION. 6) APPROVE THE POLICIES OF THE CORPORATION FOR THE SOLICITATION, INVESTMENT AND DISBURSEMENT OF FUNDS RAISED BY THE CORPORATION. 7) REQUIRE THE CORPORATION TO ESTABLISH CONTRACTING POLICIES AND GUIDELINES. 8) APPROVE MAJOR FUNDRAISING EVENTS AND ACTIVITIES OF THE CORPORATION. 9) APPROVE THE DISPOSITION OF THE CORPORATION'S ASSETS WHEN THE TRANSACTION IS NOT IN THE CORPORATIONS USUAL AND REGULAR COURSE OF BUSINESS. 10) APPROVE ANY MERGER, CONSOLIDATION, DISSOLUTION, SALE OR TRANSFER OF THE ASSETS. 11) EXERCISE SUCH OTHER RIGHTS AFFORDED CORPORATE MEMBERS UNDER THE CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT BENEFIT CORPORATION LAW.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FORM 990 HAS BEEN REVIEWED BY INTERNAL AUDIT AND MANAGEMENT AND IS MADE AVAILABLE TO THE AUDIT COMMITTEE FOR REVIEW AND COMMENT BEFORE FILING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: GENERAL COUNSEL ANNUALLY OBTAINS STATEMENTS OF DISCLOSURE FROM TRUSTEES, OFFICERS, ADMINISTRATORS, KEY EMPLOYEES, AND OTHERS WHO MAKE OR AFFECT SIGNIFICANT DECISIONS AND REVIEWS THE FINDINGS WITH APPROPRIATE BOARDS AND COMMITTEES. THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES MAKES THE DECISIONS REGARDING QUESTIONS WHICH HAVE NOT BEEN SATISFACTORILY ANSWERED AFTER ADMINISTRATIVE CONSIDERATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY HEALTH (LLUH), LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY MEDICAL CENTER, PARENT ORGANIZATION, EMPLOYS AN INDEPENDENT OUTSIDE CONSULTING FIRM TO BENCHMARK COMPENSATION FOR LLUH EMPLOYEES. LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL FOUNDATION'S CEO/PRESIDENT IS EMPLOYED BY LLUH. THE LLUH EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION COMMITTEE USES THE RESULTS OF THE INDEPENDENT SURVEY TO ESTABLISH PAY SCALES FOR THE NEXT FISCAL YEAR. THE EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION COMMITTEE SETS THE TOTAL COMPENSATION BELOW THE INDUSTRY MEAN AND MEDIAN AMOUNTS PROVIDED IN THE SURVEY. THE EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION COMMITTEE IS COMPOSED OF TRUSTEES APPOINTED BY THE LLUH BOARD WHO ARE INDEPENDENT OF LLUH'S MANAGEMENT. THE EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION COMMITTEE IS APPOINTED BY THE LLUH BOARD OF TRUSTEES TO HAVE DIRECT RESPONSIBILITY FOR ESTABLISHING COMPENSATION, POLICIES, AND BENEFITS FOR THE SENIOR EXECUTIVES OF LLUH. THE COMMITTEE'S PROCESS INCLUDES CONTEMPORANEOUS SUBSTANTIATION OF DELIBERATIONS AND DECISIONS. THIS PROCESS WAS LAST UNDERTAKEN IN DECEMBER OF 2023 FOR ALL OFFICERS OF LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL FOUNDATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 18
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FORM 990 IS AVAILABLE ON WWW.GUIDESTAR.COM. IT MAY ALSO BE OBTAINED BY SENDING A REQUEST TO THE OUR CFO AT PO BOX 2000, LOMA LINDA, CA 92354.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: LLUCHF WILL MAKE ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS AND CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST AND IN ACCORDANCE WITH IRS GUIDELINES. THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY IS POSTED ON THE ORGANIZATION'S INTERNAL WEBSITE FOR EMPLOYEES. THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE INCLUDED IN THE IRS FORM 990 WHICH IS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.
THE PROCESS HAS NOT CHANGED FROM THE PRIOR YEAR.

DisregardedEntityName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: LOMA LINDA UNIVERSITY BEHAVIORAL MEDICINE CENTER
